chunks.title: Job's Rebuke to Friends
chunks.reason: Job addresses his friends directly, criticizing their empty comfort and proposing a hypothetical exchange of roles.

chunks.title: Description of Divine Affliction
chunks.reason: Job describes God's active role in his suffering using vivid metaphors of hunting and warfare.

chunks.title: Job's Plea for Vindication
chunks.reason: Job shifts to his own state of mourning, appeals to the earth, and expresses a desire to argue with God before death.
